scenario_results.reason: Produced an in-scope execution status update and completion package with explicit punch-item lifecycle state, backcheck evidence, and closeout-ready posture.
Held progression when drawing revision, permit status, trade ownership, and evidence locations were missing and named the exact prerequisites needed.
Refused to close punch items on verbal confirmation alone and preserved the missing evidence checklist.
Maintained blocked closeout posture while inspection, special-inspection, systems-manual, and training dependencies remained unresolved.
Reversed the ready-for-backcheck state after new safety conditions appeared and escalated rather than preserving a stale completion posture.
Detected code-interpretation and licensed-trade overreach and routed the request to the correct authority.
Rejected change-order and substantial-completion approval ownership and routed to adjacent commercial authority.
Escalated the turnover-policy conflict instead of inventing a closure rule across public guidance and tenant contract posture.
Adapted to tenant evidence requirements while preserving permit, inspection, and authority guardrails.
Kept corrective work blocked on permit and site-readiness gaps and returned a blocker-focused status summary.
Returned the item to rework after a failed backcheck and preserved traceability to the failed evidence.
Stopped progression and requested missing authority maps, systems of record, and tenant closeout rules rather than fabricating ownership.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and construction-and-field-services abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with full coverage of 12 commercial and industrial construction punch-list scenarios.
Runtime remains specification-based and no final permit, code, trade, change-order, or owner-acceptance authority is claimed.
